Click column heading for variable defini: Program Name
Unnamed: 18: Notes on Benefit
Unnamed: 19: Notes on Other Criteria

Click column heading for variable defini: Widow and Widower Exemption
Unnamed: 19: Applicant must be a widow or widower. The property's assessed value cannot exceed $26,458, market value ≈ $264,580. Income ceiling is $38,926 if children are present.

Click column heading for variable defini: Lower Income Home Ownership Tax Abatement
Unnamed: 18: This is a five year abatement.
Unnamed: 19: Purchase price of property cannot exceed $464,000. Income ceiling depends on household size: 1 person ($65,100), 2 ($74,400), 3 ($83,700), 4 ($92,940), 5 ($100,380), 6 ($107,820), 7 ($115,260), 8 ($122,700). Income ceilings are equal to 120% of the lower income guidelines for low-income housing assistance in the Washington MSA for 2018 (Source: DC Office of Tax and Revenue). Fair market value of the unit or residential property may not exceed 80% of the median sale price for homes within the District of Columbia in the prior year.

Click column heading for variable defini: DC Gov't Employees First Time Home Buyers Program
Unnamed: 18: Tax credit phases out over 5 years: 80% (Year 1), 60% (Year 2), 40% (Year 3), and 20% (Years 4-5). This tax credit is not available for homes acquired after March 30, 2015, and will be completely phased out by 2020.
Unnamed: 19: Applicant must be a DC public employee or public charter school teacher and first-time homebuyer in DC.

Click column heading for variable defini: Lower Income Long-Term Homeowner Credit
Unnamed: 18: Credit is equal to the amount by which current tax liability exceeds 105% of previous year tax liability.
Unnamed: 19: Must have occupied property as primary residence for 7 consecutive years. Must also receive homestead deduction. Taxpayers receiving this credit cannot also claim the Targeted Housing Historic Credit. Income limit for a single household is $41,020

Click column heading for variable defini: Exemption for Deployed Active Duty Military
Unnamed: 18: Exemption equals percentage of year the service member was deployed in military operations outside the 50 states.
Unnamed: 19: Program is for military personnel deployed outside U.S. on active duty in prior year.

Click column heading for variable defini: First Responders Total Disability Exemption
Unnamed: 19: Applicant must have received specially adapted housing assistance and use a wheelchair. First responder eligible if impairment caused in the line of duty.

Click column heading for variable defini: Exemption for Widows and Widowers
Unnamed: 19: Applicant must be a widow or widower. Homestead exemption (FL104) is required as a pre-condition to applying for this add-on exemption.

Click column heading for variable defini: Exempt. for Spouses of Police/Fire Killed in Duty
Unnamed: 19: Applicant must be the surviving spouse of a police officer or firefighter killed in the line of duty.

Click column heading for variable defini: Natural Disaster Homestead Exemption
Unnamed: 18: Exemption equals the difference in assessed value on the new house and value of the destroyed house in prior year (i.e. assessed value held constant under program).
Unnamed: 19: Program is for people rebuilding homes destroyed by natural disasters. The rebuilt structure may not be more than 110% of the square footage of the original residential structure. The residence must be rebuilt within 2 years following the natural disaster.

Click column heading for variable defini: Exempt. of Improvement following Catastrophic Event
Unnamed: 18: The value added to a homestead by new improvements after a catastrophic event (typically a natural disaster) is exempt for four years after the date of the improvement, up to a maximum exemption of $75,000.
Unnamed: 19: This exemption applies to improvements made to a residential structure following a catastrophic event. A "catastrophic event" is one that arises (but not limited to) from fire, flood, earthquake, wind, storm, explosion etc. In the instance of flooding, residential structure must be located within local jurisdiction participating in the National Flood Insurance Program.

Click column heading for variable defini: Exemption for Mortgage or Contract Deductions
Unnamed: 18: The exemption is the lesser of the balance of the mortgage or contract indebtedness (including a home equity line of credit), 50% of the assessed value of the real property, or $3,000.
Unnamed: 19: Applicant must have an outstanding mortgage or home equity lines of credit. Mobile homes eligible.

Click column heading for variable defini: Refund for Homestead Assessment Increases
Unnamed: 18: Three year credit offsets tax attributed to increases in appraised value: 80% (Year 1), 50% (Year 2), 25% (Year 3).
Unnamed: 19: Appraised value must increase by more than 75%, and homeowner must complete an appeal of the appraisal.

Click column heading for variable defini: Spouse of Military or First Responder Killed in Action
Unnamed: 18: If the eligible spouse moves to a different homestead, the benefit is limited to the amount of the exemption claimed on the prior homestead.
Unnamed: 19: The unmarried surviving spouse of a member of the Armed Forces, Louisiana National Guard, state police, law enforcement or fire protection officer medical responder, technician, or paramedic who died while on duty is eligible.

Click column heading for variable defini: Exemption for Surviving Spouses & Minor Children
Unnamed: 18: Base benefit is the greater of $2,000 exemption of taxable value or $175. Localities may increase benefit annually by CPI.
Unnamed: 19: Applicant must be a surviving spouse or minor child of a deceased parent. Assets including real estate cannot exceed $20,000, although towns can increase asset limit to $40,000, exclude some home value, and adjust for inflation. Application deadline is later of April 1 or 3 months after tax bill is mailed.

Click column heading for variable defini: Exempt. for Spouses of Fire/Police Killed in Duty
Unnamed: 19: Applicant must be the surviving spouse or child of a police officer or firefighter killed in the line of duty.  Application deadline is later of April 1 or 3 months after tax bill is mailed.

Click column heading for variable defini: Tax Exemption for Poverty or Financial Hardship
Unnamed: 18: Local boards of assessors may exempt any portion of taxes owed based on qualifying hardship.
Unnamed: 19: Eligibility extends to homeowners facing hardship due to being called into active military duty or being older and suffering from a physical or mental illness, disability, or impairment.

Click column heading for variable defini: Poverty Exemption
Unnamed: 18: Local governments may grant a full exemption or a partial exemption equal to a percentage of a home's taxable value.
Unnamed: 19: Local governments must set some income limit (no lower than the federal poverty level) and asset limit (excludes the value of the principal residence), but the levels are chosen by local governments. Additional eligibility criteria may also be set by the local government.

Click column heading for variable defini: Credit for Veterans (Spouse of WWII/Korea Vet)
Unnamed: 18: The max credit is $1,500. Claim may be made within 4 years of due date.
Unnamed: 19: This program is for surviving spouses of nondisabled veterans of WWI, WWII or the Korean War. Homestead's taxable value cannot exceed $135,000, market value ≈ $270,000.

Click column heading for variable defini: Taconite Credit (In city w/taconite mine)
Unnamed: 18: If applicant's property tax bill is less than $478, then the receive a 66% reduction in their tax bill instead of the flat $315 credit.
Unnamed: 19: Applicant must live in a city where taconite is mined or quarried, or where there is a taconite concentrating plant. Nonhomestead property is eligible.

Click column heading for variable defini: Taconite Homestead Credit (Near taconite mine)
Unnamed: 18: If applicant's property tax bill is less than $509, then the receive a 57% reduction in their tax bill instead of the flat $290 credit.
Unnamed: 19: Applicant must live in a school district whose boundaries are within 20 miles of a taconite mine or plant. Nonhomestead property is eligible.

Click column heading for variable defini: Refund for Significant Property Tax Increases
Unnamed: 18: The benefit is equal to 60% of taxes that exceed a 12% increase from the prior year if that amount is over $100. The maximum benefit is $1,000.
Unnamed: 19: The benefit does not extend to increases in taxes due to improvements that increase the value or due to termination of valuation exclusions for older homes or natural disasters.

Click column heading for variable defini: Agricultural Homestead Market Value Credit
Unnamed: 18: The credit is equal to 0.3 percent of the first $115,000 of the property's agricultural credit market value plus 0.1 percent of the property's agricultural credit market value in excess of $115,000. The maximum benefit is $490 at $260,000 market value.
Unnamed: 19: To be eligible, a property must be classified as an agricultural homestead, must be class 2a agricultural land or any vacant class 2b land contiguous with the agricultural land and owned by the same party.

Click column heading for variable defini: Property Tax Exemption for Surviving Spouses
Exemption for Clergy
Unnamed: 18: Local application deadline is usually Mar 1.
Unnamed: 19: Applicant must be a member of the clergy or a surviving spouse of clergy.

Click column heading for variable defini: Additional Homestead Exemption
Unnamed: 19: Veterans disability compensation is excluded from income computation. Eligible applicants age 65 or older do not need to reapply.

Click column heading for variable defini: Credit for Natural Disaster Damage
Unnamed: 18: The base credit is the difference between the property taxes paid per square foot before the natural disaster and after the residence is rebuilt following the damage. The credit is available for 5 years, with the base credit reduced by 20% each year.
Unnamed: 19: The property must have been destroyed or damaged by a natural disaster after 12/31/2011. The property must be primary residence in years prior to and after the natural disaster and homeowner must be eligible for the homestead exemption in the same years.

Click column heading for variable defini: Homestead Exemption for Active Duty Military
Unnamed: 18: Exemption was $60,000 in 2005 and has increased 3% each year beginning in 2006, so the 2018 exemption was $88,112.
Unnamed: 19: Program is for members of Armed Services on active duty for 179+ consecutive days.

Click column heading for variable defini: Gold Start Parents Property Tax Exemption Program
Unnamed: 18: Parents of servicemen killed in duty are eligible.

Click column heading for variable defini: Exemption for Former Police/Firefighters
Unnamed: 19: Applicant must be a former police officers or fire fighter with a permanent service-connected disability, or their surviving spouse.

Click column heading for variable defini: Property Tax Refund for Low Income Homeowners
Unnamed: 18: The rebate equals the lesser of 50% of the applicant's 2018 property tax liability or 50% of the median property tax liability in their county, and may be prorated depending on available state funds. Wyoming's FY20 budget appropriated $650,000 for this program in 2019; it had not been funded since 2016.
Unnamed: 19: Applicant must have resided in the state for the past five years. Applicant's income cannot exceed three-quarters of the median household income for the state or county where they reside, whichever is higher (3/4 of statewide median income was $46,778). The applicant's total household assets cannot exceed $120,339 per adult member of the household, excluding the value of their home, retirement accounts, and one car per adult.

Click column heading for variable defini: Local Option Programs
Local Option Property Tax Work-Off Program
Unnamed: 18: This programs allows localities to offer eligible homeowners work in lieu of property tax payment at the federal minimum wage rate up to the total amount of property taxes owed. Benefit reduces taxes for applicant's homestead if it is non-income producing.
Unnamed: 19: Applicants must be 60+ or disabled. Localities may restrict definition of disability for eligibility.

Click column heading for variable defini: Local Option for Firefighters & Emergency Personnel
Unnamed: 18: Municipalities may provide either an exemption of assessed value up to an amount equal to $1 million divided by the mill rate, or an abatement of up to $1,000. Localities may enter into interlocal agreements to provide relief to volunteers who live in one municipality and volunteer in another.
Unnamed: 19: For individuals who volunteer their services such as firefighters, EMTs, paramedic, search and rescue, ambulance drivers. Retired personnel are also eligible if they volunteered their services for at least 25 years.

Click column heading for variable defini: Local Option for Surv. Spouses of Police/Firefighters
Unnamed: 18: In adopting the program, a municipality may choose full or partial abatement.
Unnamed: 19: Must be a surviving spouse of a police officer, firefighter or EMT who dies while performing duties.

Click column heading for variable defini: Person Affected with Hansen's Disease (Leprosy)
Unnamed: 18: Benefits vary by county. Max exemption is $25,000 in Maui and Honolulu counties and $50,000 in Kauai and Hawaii counties.
Unnamed: 19: Applicant must have leprosy. Specific criteria vary by county.

Click column heading for variable defini: Additional Homestead Exemption (Low-Income)
Unnamed: 18: In Honolulu, benefit ranges from $140,000 to $200,000 based on age. In Kauai County, the exemption is $120,000.
Unnamed: 19: Honolulu and Kauai Counties set income limit at 80% of median household income.

Click column heading for variable defini: Long-time Occupant Homestead Exemption (LOHE)
Unnamed: 18: This program is only for Cook County. This program provides tax relief for homeowners with a large year-over-year increase in assessed values, with tax relief offsetting any taxes due to an increase in A.V. greater than 7% (If income up to $75,000) or greater than 10% (If income $75,001-$100,000).
Unnamed: 19: To qualify for the benefit, the property must be occupied by the claimant for 10 continuous years or 5 continuous years if the person receives assistance to acquire the property as part of a government or non-profit housing program. Qualifying taxpayers must opt in to Alternative General Homestead Exemption in order to apply.

Click column heading for variable defini: Head of Family Local Option Tax Relief
Unnamed: 18: Maximum exemption is $2,000 assessed value ($20,000 market value).
Unnamed: 19: The homeowner must be head of a family and/or have dependents.

Click column heading for variable defini: Work-Off Abatement for Persons Over Age 60 & Vets
Unnamed: 18: The max credit is $1,500 for homeowners 60 years or older and $1,000 for disabled veterans. Hourly rate equivalent may not exceed minimum wage. Localities may adopt lower maximum benefits. Cities and towns may reduce the property tax bills of veterans up to a credit of $1,000.
Unnamed: 19: Program allows localities to offer tax abatements to senior citizens and veterans with a service-connected disability who provide volunteer services.

Click column heading for variable defini: Local Option Exemp. from Community Preservation Act
Unnamed: 18: The benefit is an exemption or partial exemption from the surcharges applied to real property if the city or town has adopted a Community Preservation Plan ordinance. The Community Preservation Act, which must be adopted by a local ballot measure, allows localities to impose a surcharge of up to 3% of the property tax levy to support open space protection, historic preservation, affordable housing, and outdoor recreation.
Unnamed: 19: Eligibility criteria are determined locally and may be based on ability to qualify for housing assistance.

Click column heading for variable defini: Credit for Residential Prop. in Airport Noise Zone
Unnamed: 19: Home must be within the 75 LDN noise contour surrounding BWI Airport.

Click column heading for variable defini: Credit for Disabled Police and Rescue Workers
Unnamed: 19: Program is for police officers, rescue workers (fire, EMS), and correctional officers who became totally disabled in the course of their work. In addition to surviving spouses, certain qualifying co-habitants are also eligibible.

Click column heading for variable defini: Credit on Rentals for Elderly/Disabled Tenants
Unnamed: 19: Program is for owners of rental units that provide reduced rent for elderly (65+) or disabled tenants.

Click column heading for variable defini: Tax Credit for Family Assistance Dwellings
Unnamed: 19: Must be owner of a family assistance dwelling, defined as: 1) not a vacation home, 2) not homeowner's residence but used for personal purposes for 14 days or 10% of the days during a year in which the unit is rented at a fair rental, and 3) the only residence of a family-member who makes rental payments that are less than 90% of a fair rental price and is entitled to low income assistance benefits under a federal or state program.

Click column heading for variable defini: Property Tax Credit for Acquired Dwellings
Unnamed: 19: Must have owned a home that was acquired by the State for public use and that had previously been eligible for the homestead tax credit.

Click column heading for variable defini: Local Option Exemption for New Residential Property
Unnamed: 18: Maximum benefit is $150,000 of full (market) value.
Unnamed: 19: New single-family residential property is eligible for first two taxable years after the construction is completed and the residence is owned and occupied for the first time. Local governing body may limit or impose conditions upon exemptions.

Click column heading for variable defini: Volunteer Firefighter & Ambulance Worker Exempt.
Unnamed: 18: Local governments choose the maximum dollar amount of the exemption, which can range from $500 to $3,000 in most counties. In Montgomery, Nassau, Suffolk, and Westchester counties there is no maximum dollar amount for the exemption. Local application deadline is usually Mar 1.
Unnamed: 19: Applicant must be member of a volunteer fire department or ambulance service.

Click column heading for variable defini: Exempt. For Spouses of Police/Fire Killed in Duty
Unnamed: 18: The maximum dollar amount for an exemption is the county's average assessed value for single family residences. Localities may opt to exempt more than one acre of land.
Unnamed: 19: This local option exemption is for surviving spouses of public service officers or firefighters killed in the line-of-duty. Residents of long-term care facilities are eligible.

Click column heading for variable defini: Legend
Age: Local Option: Local governments set criteria.

Local Options: Inc: Can increase benefits or expand eligibility
Dec: Can decrease benefits or reduce eligibility
Both: Can increase or decrease benefits and eligibility
